children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: For purposes of this title, the term “certified professional employer organization” means a person who applies to be treated as a certified professional employer organization for purposes of section 3511 and has been certified by the Secretary as meeting the requirements of subsection (b).

demonstrates that such person (and any owner, officer, and other persons as may be specified in regulations) meets such requirements as the Secretary shall establish, including requirements with respect to tax status, background, experience, business location, and annual financial audits,
agrees that it will satisfy the bond and independent financial review requirements of subsection (c) on an ongoing basis,
agrees that it will satisfy such reporting obligations as may be imposed by the Secretary,
computes its taxable income using an accrual method of accounting unless the Secretary approves another method,
agrees to verify on such periodic basis as the Secretary may prescribe that it continues to meet the requirements of this subsection, and

agrees to notify the Secretary in writing within such time as the Secretary may prescribe of any change that materially affects the continuing accuracy of any agreement or information that was previously made or provided under this subsection.

children.heading: Bond and independent financial review
children.label: c Bond and independent financial review
A
children.text: meets the bond requirements of paragraph (2), and

children.label: B
children.text: meets the independent financial review requirements of paragraph (3).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A certified professional employer organization meets the requirements of this paragraph if the organization has posted a bond for the payment of taxes under subtitle C (in a form acceptable to the Secretary) that is in an amount at least equal to the amount specified in subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: 5 percent of the organization’s liability under section 3511 for taxes imposed by subtitle C during the preceding calendar year (but not to exceed $1,000,000), or

children.label: A
children.text: has, as of the most recent audit date, caused to be prepared and provided to the Secretary (in such manner as the Secretary may prescribe) an opinion of an independent certified public accountant as to whether the certified professional employer organization’s financial statements are presented fairly in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles, and

children.label: B
children.text: provides to the Secretary an assertion regarding Federal employment tax payments and an examination level attestation on such assertion from an independent certified public accountant not later than the last day of the second month beginning after the end of each calendar quarter.

children.heading: Controlled group rules
children.label: 4 Controlled group rules
children.text: For purposes of the requirements of paragraphs (2) and (3), all certified professional employer organizations that are members of a controlled group within the meaning of sections 414(b) and (c) shall be treated as a single organization.

children.heading: Failure to file assertion and attestation
children.label: 5 Failure to file assertion and attestation
children.text: If the certified professional employer organization fails to file the assertion and attestation required by paragraph (3) with respect to any calendar quarter, then the requirements of paragraph (3) with respect to such failure shall be treated as not satisfied for the period beginning on the due date for such attestation.

children.heading: Audit date
children.label: 6 Audit date
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (3)(A), the audit date shall be six months after the completion of the organization’s fiscal year.

children.heading: Suspension and revocation authority
children.label: d Suspension and revocation authority
children.text: The Secretary may suspend or revoke a certification of any person under subsection (b) for purposes of section 3511 if the Secretary determines that such person is not satisfying the agreements or requirements of subsections (b) or (c), or fails to satisfy applicable accounting, reporting, payment, or deposit requirements.

children.label: A
children.text: performs services for a customer pursuant to a contract which is between such customer and the certified professional employer organization and which meets the requirements of paragraph (2), and

children.label: B
children.text: performs services at a work site meeting the requirements of paragraph (3).

children.label: A
children.text: assume responsibility for payment of wages to such individual, without regard to the receipt or adequacy of payment from the customer for such services,

children.label: B
children.text: assume responsibility for reporting, withholding, and paying any applicable taxes under subtitle C, with respect to such individual’s wages, without regard to the receipt or adequacy of payment from the customer for such services,

children.label: C
children.text: assume responsibility for any employee benefits which the service contract may require the certified professional employer organization to provide, without regard to the receipt or adequacy of payment from the customer for such benefits,

children.label: D
children.text: assume responsibility for recruiting, hiring, and firing workers in addition to the customer’s responsibility for recruiting, hiring, and firing workers,

children.label: E
children.text: maintain employee records relating to such individual, and

children.label: F
children.text: agree to be treated as a certified professional employer organization for purposes of section 3511 with respect to such individual.

children.heading: Work site coverage requirement
children.label: 3 Work site coverage requirement
children.text: The requirements of this paragraph are met with respect to an individual if at least 85 percent of the individuals performing services for the customer at the work site where such individual performs services are subject to 1 or more contracts with the certified professional employer organization which meet the requirements of paragraph (2) (but not taking into account those individuals who are excluded employees within the meaning of section 414(q)(5)).
each person certified as a professional employer organization under subsection (a), and
each person whose certification as a professional employer organization is suspended or revoked under subsection (d).

children.heading: Determination of employment status
children.label: g Determination of employment status
children.text: Except to the extent necessary for purposes of section 3511, nothing in this section shall be construed to affect the determination of who is an employee or employer for purposes of this title.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: h Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section.
